title: Delphinastrum glaucum
taxonomy: Delphinastrum glaucum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family includes a wide range of plants, including cabbages, broccoli, and mustard greens. The Brassicaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, but the exact classification of Delphinastrum glaucum within this family is more specific. Delphinastrum glaucum is a member of the subfamily Brassicoideae, which is one of the most diverse and widespread subfamilies within the family Brassicaceae. The classification of Delphinastrum glaucum is also supported by its morphological and genetic characteristics, which are typical of plants in the Brassicaceae family.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage.
